sentences.text: Gabrielė Vilkickytė, a performer and songwriter, says that when she was not yet daring to create, she used to perform the song "Tamsus langas" at concerts.

Although they did not manage to sing this piece together with Alvydas Lukoševičius-Obuolis, technology will help to make this and other impossible duets come true in the musical talk show "Dream Duets", which starts on Saturday on LRT TELEVISION.
"I once saw three hours of footage from my childhood.
And there she is - three-year-old Gabrielytė running around the apartment where we lived.

I look at this child for three hours and think: I'll do anything for this child", says G. Vilkickytė, one of the five participants of the first "Dream Duets" show, about her determination to pursue a career as a musician.

According to the singer, at the beginning of her career, the song "Tamsus langas" by A. Lukoševičius-Obuolis was very important to her: "That song was a cocoon of security for me in my childhood, I found comfort in it.
Then I would include that song in my programme - before I dared to write it myself.
For me, it was a song that I would have written myself, but I didn't have time because he had already written it down."

G. Vilkickytė has chosen this particular song for her dream duet: she will go to A. Lukoševičius' performance with the help of technology and on Saturday she will see her dream duet for the first time together with the viewers of LRT TELEVISION.
The show will also feature four more music videos and candid stories about the participants' lives and careers.

Advertising specialist and performer Dovilė Filmanavičiūtė will take a look at the music video for the song "Dancing on My Own" by Danish artist Robyn and explain why it was so important at the beginning of her personal music career.
Sasha Song will join the legendary George Michael on the legendary song "Freedom" to share the story of what freedom meant to him at the most difficult time of his life.
The audience will also see Vaidas Baumila's dream duet on air - he will appear in the music video for John Legend's song "Ordinary People".

Rolandas Janavičius, who has a bright memory, will also appear, and will be joined by Gintarė Karaliūnaitė, who will sing the song "Rytas perone" (Morning on the platform), which recalls the beginning of their long-lasting friendship.
Talk about life's difficulties overcome, inspiration to create and duets that until now seemed impossible in the music talk show "Dream Duets".
The first episode of the project will be broadcast on LRT TELEVISION and on LRT.lt this Saturday at 9 pm.
After the premiere, each programme will also be available for viewing in the LRT media centre.
Read more about the new season of LRT TELEVISION and LRT PLUS on the dedicated page.
